steps.title: Identify the consequence relationship
steps.content: The first sentence states a limitation: Curiosity couldn't preserve samples. The second sentence describes NASA's response when building Perseverance: they sought to implement a mechanism that could do exactly that. This phrase ties the new rover's design directly to overcoming Curiosity's limitation—a classic cause-and-effect signal.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Thus correctly introduces the consequence: because Curiosity lacked the ability to preserve samples, NASA created Perseverance to address that specific gap. The other three fail to convey this cause-and-effect relationship, instead suggesting time reversals, elaborations, or false parallels.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
